activity_windows.description: Time windows for activity status calculations

discovery_activity_window_days.description: Days to consider for discovery activity

zombie_window_days.description: Days of inactivity before asset is considered stale for zombie classification

default_activity_window_days.description: Default activity window for shadow/zombie classification

finance_thresholds.description: Financial thresholds for admission and findings

minimum_spend.description: Minimum monthly spend (USD) for finance gate admission

finance_gap_monthly_threshold.description: Minimum monthly spend (USD) to trigger FINANCE_GAP finding

finance_gap_annual_threshold.description: Minimum annual spend (USD) to trigger FINANCE_GAP finding

noise_floor.description: Minimum number of discovery sources required for admission

require_valid_ci_type.description: Require valid CI type for CMDB-based admission

require_valid_lifecycle.description: Require valid lifecycle status for CMDB admission

min_discovery_sources_for_shadow.description: Minimum corroborating sources for shadow classification

allow_finance_only_admission.description: Allow finance-only admission without governance or discovery corroboration

enable_vendor_propagation.description: Enable vendor governance propagation (IdP/CMDB from vendor siblings)

finance_requires_discovery.description: Require discovery evidence for finance-based admission (set false to reduce rejections)

require_corroboration.description: Require 2+ discovery sources for discovery-only admission (set false to honor noise_floor=1)

stale_window_days.description: Days of inactivity before asset is considered stale

include_infra.description: Include infrastructure assets in discovery results

treat_directory_as_idp.description: Treat directory services (AD, LDAP) as IdP

use_policy_engine.description: Use new policy engine for admission decisions

late_binding_domain_merge.description: Enable late-binding domain merge for multi-domain assets

idp_governance.description: IdP governance matching policy - controls how IdP matches assert governance

trust_heuristic_matches.description: Whether heuristic IdP matches (fuzzy, name, vendor) can assert governance. FALSE (strict) = only domain-based matches grant governance, heuristic matches are enrichment-only (Farm's approach - never miss shadow IT). TRUE (loose) = heuristic matches CAN grant governance (reduces noise for customers with messy IdP data, but may hide shadow IT risks).

heuristic_requires_sso.description: When trust_heuristic_matches=true, require SSO for heuristic matches to grant governance. TRUE = heuristic matches only govern if IdP has SSO enabled. FALSE = heuristic matches govern even without SSO.

fuzzy_matching.description: Parameters for fuzzy name matching in correlation

max_edit_distance.description: Maximum edit distance for fuzzy name matching

min_name_length.description: Minimum name length to apply fuzzy matching

max_confidence.description: Maximum confidence for vendor hypothesis (never fully authoritative)

max_observation_samples.description: Maximum observation samples to store per run

infrastructure_domain_handling.description: Policy for handling infrastructure and vendor root domains. Infrastructure domains are shared multi-tenant services that are NOT enterprise-owned assets. Excluding them is a modeling choice, not a shortcut.

mode.description: How to handle infrastructure domains: 'exclude' = never catalog or classify, 'observe_only' = visible in catalog but never classified as shadow/zombie

shared_infrastructure_domains.description: CDNs, static asset hosts, and internet plumbing. Shared delivery planes used by thousands of applications - traffic to these does NOT mean the enterprise uses them as an application.

vendor_root_portals.description: Vendor homepages and generic login portals. Not tenant-specific, not deployable by the enterprise. These are front doors, not systems.

dev_build_infrastructure.description: Developer and build infrastructure. Open source project sites, package managers, and dev tools - not SaaS applications.

generic_collision_roots.description: High-collision eTLD+1 domains that are likely extraction artifacts, not owned assets. If unanchored (no CMDB/IdP/Finance/Cloud), these are suppressed. If anchored, allowed but tagged RISK_GENERIC_DOMAIN=true.

key_strategy_version.description: Key generation strategy version. v1=current (domains[0]), v2=provenance-aware priority (discovery > cmdb > idp > name)

custom_exclusions.description: Operator-managed domain exclusions for tenant-specific noise filtering

domains.description: Custom domains to exclude from discovery. Operator-managed for tenant-specific noise reduction.

corporate_root_domains.description: Customer corporate domains to exclude from shadow classification

domains.description: Customer's own corporate marketing and root domains. These should not appear as shadow IT.

connection_policy.description: AAM connection gating policy - determines which findings block asset connection

identity_gap.description: No SSO integration - no lifecycle control, no offboarding, no access auditability

finance_gap.description: Active spend without accountable owner - dangerous to enable integrations

data_conflict.description: Sources disagree on identity/ownership - cannot safely act

zombie_asset.description: Registered but inactive - requires human judgment on value

duplication_risk.description: Potential overlap with another asset - confirmation needed

cmdb_gap.description: Asset not in CMDB - hygiene issue only, everyone has this

webhook_url.description: Farm webhook URL for policy change notifications

auto_notify_on_change.description: Automatically notify Farm when policy changes

sync_interval_seconds.description: Periodic sync interval (0 = disabled, Farm pulls on demand)
